figures.caption: Fig. 2: End-to-end inference throughput (images/s) at 50% pruning, DeiT-Base. Our Triton pipeline matches FA2 varlen at large batch sizes and outperforms padded SDPA by up to 2.24×.

Fig. 4: Accuracy–throughput Pareto front on ImageNet-1K (DeiTS). The Triton backend strictly dominates: at 81.7% top-1 (25% pruning), throughput is 4,162 img/s—exceeding the unpruned baseline (3,780 img/s) while losing only 0.5% accuracy.

TABLE I: Attention kernel latency (ms) on DeiT-Base, A100. FA2 varlen’s latency is nearly constant (∼0.063 ms) regardless of pruning ratio, revealing a dispatch-overhead floor. Our Triton kernel’s lower floor (∼0.040 ms) lets pruning savings manifest in wall-clock time. Bold: fastest per row.

Fig. 1: Speedup vs. prune ratio (BS=64, DeiT-Base, A100). Padded execution yields no speedup at any sparsity level (flat at ∼ 0.75×), while our ragged pipeline tracks the theoretical curve, reaching up to 2.13× at 90%.
